understanding.technical_notes:
Assignee: Oleksii Samusenko Reporter: Elgin Ihloff Created: Thu, 29 Jan 2026 13:06:28 +0100 Updated: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:51:15 +0100 Fix Version: UVZ future Resolution: Unresolved Comments (4): [Comment 1] JIRAUSER62328 (Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:47:02 +0100):

took step back to look onto problems described by Krystian Kościukiewicz from different angle, found couple of simpler solution for some of them, here is what was able to achieve (together with further planned steps):

Migration plan:

  • update angular.json to be compatible with new builder
  • come up with alternative approach to loading xlf file with translations via webpack.raw-loader (used to setup translations for ng-boostrap)
    changes:
    • switched to raw import (https://vite.dev/guide/assets#importing-asset-as-string)
    • configured loader for xlf files in angular.json
    • added '.xlf?raw' module type definition to satisfy tscompiler
  • configure sass-compiler to silence deprecation warnings
  • convert custom webpack config to esbuild script for jsapi subproject in progress
  • verify if new setup is working in:
    • dev mode phase 1 New Angular Version (Vite for for development server - ng serve)
      • ESM module graph server
      • Very strict
      • Injects HMR scripts that must not be reverse-proxied
      • Requires exclusive control of 4200
      • Cannot serve arbitrary JS without transforming them
      • Cannot be customized
      • Cannot run behind Nginx without breaking dev modules
      • Must handle only Angular application code
    • Old Angular Version (Webpack DevServer)
      • Static server
      • Flexible
      • OK with arbitrary JS
      • No special HMR ESM modules
      • Didn’t require exclusive control of 4200
      • Could serve jsApi bundles “for free”
  • jsapi module adjusments for esbuild
  • jsApi configuration adapted for the compilerOptions
  • memory increase for start/build/build:endpoint -> xnp-local starters due to higher usage on esbuild

Open Point :

  • Xnp loads : https://localhost/uvz/v1/web/index.html, and nginx forwards this to Angular 19 Builder + Vite Migration

    [Comment 4] JIRAUSER59240 (Tue, 3 Feb 2026 09:15:26 +0100):

    This feature is really about amigrating to the "esbuilder" after the angular migration

    Findings: 

    • Remove webpack.config.js – esbuild does NOT use Webpack configs
      • The esbuild does not read any of these:
        • webpack.config.js (todo)
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • module.rules (check) 
        • loaders, plugins, etc. (check)
      • If your subproject’s Webpack config is only doing SCSS → CSS via sass-loader.  
        Angular 19 already handles SCSS automatically.  (todo) 
      • Move your SCSS configuration into angular.json (todo)
      • Angular’s new build system handles SCSS via the (todo) stylePreprocessorOptions.sass section, where you can configure include paths and Sass behavior. This is the same place where Angular 19 introduced options for controlling Sass deprecation warnings (e.g. silenceDeprecations).

     

    • Remove sass-loader entirely (Angular 19 uses Dart Sass internally)
      • Old setup likely included:
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • node-sass (deprecated) or sass (todo)
        • resolve-url-loader (check) 
        •  These are not needed anymore.
          Angular 19 uses Dart Sass 1.80.x internally

     

    •  jsApi migration options (todo for the module)
      • jsApi subproject is it with own build and dev server. 

     

    • Option A (fastest / lowest risk): keep jsApi on its current toolchain
      • Recommended if jsApi is only loosely coupled to the Angular app.
      • Keep existing Webpack/Vite/Rollup/etc. pipeline as-is
      • Expose it via npm scripts (and possibly an ng target that just runs a command)
      • Keep Angular migra

migration_sequence.title: Standalone components are now default
migration_steps: Run schematic ng generate @angular/core:standalone-migration
Review generated standalone components for correctness
Update module imports to reflect standalone usage

migration_sequence.title: node-sass -> Dart Sass (sass)
migration_steps: Remove node-sass from package.json
Install sass (Dart Sass) as devDependency
Update angular.json builder configuration to use @angular-devkit/build-angular:application
Silence deprecation warnings via sass options

migration_sequence.title: Browser builder -> Application builder
migration_steps: Replace "builder": "@angular-devkit/build-angular:browser" with "@angular-devkit/build-angular:application" in angular.json
Adjust serve options to match new builder schema
Validate that custom webpack configurations are migrated to esbuild where needed

migration_sequence.title: TypeScript 5.4 -> 5.6
migration_steps: Update typescript version in package.json to ^5.6
Run tsc to identify any type errors
Fix any incompatibilities introduced by TS 5.6

migration_sequence.title: Zone.js 0.14 -> 0.15
migration_steps: Update zone.js version in package.json to ^0.15
Run application and verify change detection works as expected

migration_sequence.title: ngIf/ngFor/ngSwitch -> @if/@for/@switch control flow
migration_steps: Run schematic ng generate @angular/core:control-flow-migration
Manually verify transformed templates for correctness

migration_sequence.title: APP_INITIALIZER -> provideAppInitializer
migration_steps: Replace APP_INITIALIZER providers with provideAppInitializer calls
Adjust imports accordingly

migration_sequence.title: Karma test runner -> Vitest/Web Test Runner
migration_steps: Remove karma and related plugins
Install vitest and @angular-devkit/build-angular:dev-server
Migrate test configuration files to Vitest format
Update test scripts in package.json

migration_sequence.title: AG Grid compatibility check
migration_steps: Verify current AG Grid version compatibility with Angular 19
Upgrade AG Grid if required
Run grid related unit/integration tests

migration_sequence.title: ng-bootstrap compatibility check
migration_steps: Check ng-bootstrap version against Angular 19 support matrix
Upgrade ng-bootstrap if needed
Run UI component tests

migration_sequence.title: ngx-translate compatibility check
migration_steps: Validate ngx-translate version compatibility
Upgrade if necessary
Test translation loading (including raw-loader replacement)

migration_sequence.title: @Input() -> input() signal
migration_steps: Run schematic ng generate @angular/core:signal-input-migration
Review migrated inputs for correct signal usage

migration_sequence.title: @ViewChild -> viewChild() signal query
migration_steps: Run schematic ng generate @angular/core:signal-queries-migration
Validate viewChild signal queries in components

migration_sequence.title: @Output() -> output() signal
migration_steps: Run schematic ng generate @angular/core:output-migration
Check event emission via output signals

migration_sequence.title: HttpClientModule -> provideHttpClient()
migration_steps: Replace HttpClientModule imports with provideHttpClient() in providers array
Verify HTTP services still function

pre_migration_checks: Run full test suite and record baseline coverage
Backup current branch and ensure CI pipeline is green
Confirm all third‑party library versions support Angular 19
post_migration_checks: Execute ng build for production and verify no compilation errors
Run full test suite (unit + integration) and compare against baseline
Perform manual smoke test of critical user flows

implementation_steps: 2. Replace node-sass with sass and configure deprecation silencing (SharedModule)
3. Migrate raw-loader usage for .xlf files to ES module raw imports (CoreModule)
4. Convert custom webpack config for jsApi subproject to esbuild script (AdaptersModule)
5. Apply standalone migration schematic and adjust module imports (All modules)
6. Upgrade TypeScript to 5.6 and resolve type errors (CoreModule)
7. Update zone.js to 0.15 and verify change detection (WorkflowModule)
9. Migrate tests from Karma to Vitest and adjust test scripts (All test suites)
10. Run control‑flow migration for ngIf/ngFor/ngSwitch (All components)
11. Upgrade AG Grid, ng-bootstrap, ngx-translate to versions compatible with Angular 19 (SharedModule, OnlineHelpModule)
12. Migrate @Input, @ViewChild, @Output to signal APIs using schematics (All UI components)
14. Execute full build and run integration tests in dev mode (Dev environment)
15. Document migration changes and update ADR if necessary

unit_tests: Run existing Jasmine unit tests after migration to ensure component logic remains unchanged
Add unit tests for any newly introduced signal APIs
integration_tests: Execute end‑to‑end tests covering translation loading, API communication, and UI interactions
Validate that jsApi proxy works with the new esbuild server
similar_patterns: XnpPdfAdapter (jasmine, unit, relevance: 0.116) – use as reference for structuring unit tests after migration

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (cors in ActionRestService, AbstractSecurityConfiguration, JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl)
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for state‑changing operations (csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration)
Apply pre_authorize checks where needed (pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService)
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotations to DeedEntry fields (not_null on DeedEntry)
Add @Valid annotation to DeedEntryRestService methods (valid on DeedEntryRestService)
Add @NotBlank, @Min, @Max annotations to KmConfig properties (not_blank, min, max on KmConfig)
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException types for domain errors (RuntimeException custom_exception)
Add specific @ExceptionHandler for HttpClientErrorException and HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The migration touches core build configuration, multiple Angular modules, and introduces new tooling (esbuild, Vitest). While individual steps are straightforward, the breadth of affected components and need for extensive testing raise overall complexity.
risks: Potential regressions in UI due to SASS import deprecation
Build failures caused by mismatched builder configuration
Test runner migration breaking existing test suites
